label: Iran Killed
source: Hengaw / HRANA / Iran Health Ministry / Al Jazeera / Iran Red Crescent
contestNote: Hengaw 7th report (Mar 25): 6,530 total killed including 640 confirmed civilians. HRANA: 3,461+ killed incl. 1,551 civilians, 236 children. Iran Red Crescent: 1,900–1,937+ killed, 24,800+ injured. Iran Health Ministry: 210+ children killed. WHO: 18 hospitals struck; 190 health/medical centers hit; 17 Red Cross centers struck; ~100 ambulances damaged. 3.2M Iranians displaced (UNHCR). Internet blackout (~1% connectivity / 350+ hrs) severely limits verification. Day 32 (Mar 31): IDF struck 170+ targets over Tehran, Isfahan, Shiraz, Parchin — drone factories, warhead casting plants, military HQ. 11,000+ total targets struck since Feb 28.

label: US Killed
deltaNote: 13 KIA confirmed. Total wounded now 300+. Iran struck Prince Sultan AB (Saudi Arabia, Mar 28) — 15 US wounded (5 seriously), 2 E-3 Sentry aircraft damaged. USS Tripoli (3,500 Marines) arrived Mar 29. 82nd Airborne (~1,000+) in CENTCOM AOR. CENTCOM: Iran missile rate down 90% from Day 1; total 393 attack waves recorded. 11,000+ US/Israeli targets struck in Iran. Apr 6 Hormuz deadline firm.

label: Israel Killed
source: IDF Spokesperson / Israeli health authorities / Times of Israel
deltaNote: 19+ killed in Israel total. 4,800+ wounded. Mar 28 (Day 29): Iranian missile struck Eshtaol (Jerusalem area) — IDF interception failure; several lightly injured. Houthi missile toward Beersheba triggered sirens. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rockets; IDF Chief Zamir warns of 15,000-troop shortfall. Iran's pre-stockpiled missiles still penetrating Israeli defenses despite production destroyed.

label: Gulf Region Killed
deltaNote: 22+ total across Gulf states. Mar 24: Iran struck fuel tank at Kuwait International Airport, sparking fire; Saudi Arabia and UAE reportedly 'losing patience' and may join strikes on Iran (Bloomberg). UAE Shah Gas Field attacked Mar 18 (suspended operations). Qatar LNG remains halted Day 27. Saudi Arabia intercepted 37+ drones. 16+ vessels struck in Persian Gulf.

label: Lebanon Killed
source: Lebanese health authorities / Lebanon Information Ministry
deltaNote: 1,142+ killed including 130+ children; 1M+ displaced. Mar 28: IDF struck Beirut southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) and Deir al-Zahrani; Al Manar/Al Mayadeen journalists killed; Lebanese President Aoun condemned strikes. Mar 27: 1 IDF soldier killed by Lebanese rocket fire. Mar 25: Hezbollah fired 100+ rockets. IDF has declared 30km security zone to Litani River.

label: Brent Crude
source: Reuters / Al Jazeera / OilPrice.com / CNBC / Goldman Sachs / Bloomberg
deltaNote: Brent ~$107/bbl Day 32 (down from $115 Day 31) amid back-channel diplomacy optimism. WTI ~$104. US gas hits $4.02/gal first time since 2022; California $5.87/gal. Analyst Fesharaki (Bloomberg): oil could reach $150–$200 if Hormuz stays shut 6–8 more weeks. 'Every week 100M bbl not going through.' Apr 6 Hormuz deadline firm. Iran struck VLCC Al Salmi at Dubai Port (Day 32).

label: Flights Cancelled
deltaNote: ~500+ tankers stranded in Persian Gulf. Qatar LNG offline (Day 2) — global LNG and fertilizer supply chains disrupted. Hormuz selective passage: Iran allowing China/India/Pakistan-flagged vessels under coordination. 20M bbl/day backed up; 5M diverted to alternate routes. UN FAO emergency alert on food price spike. European natural gas TTF +22% cumulative since war onset. Slovenia: first EU fuel rationing. Spain: €5B emergency energy aid.

label: Countries Affected
source: Al Jazeera / Reuters / France 24 / CNN / NewsNation
deltaNote: Iran, Israel, Lebanon, UAE (VLCC Al Salmi struck at Dubai Port Day 32), Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia (Prince Sultan AB struck), Oman, Iraq, Jordan, Yemen (Houthis), Azerbaijan, Turkey, Cyprus, France, Switzerland, Pakistan (intermediary), Slovenia, Thailand, Malaysia. Day 32: Iran struck VLCC in Dubai port; IDF Day 32 strikes hit 170+ targets; Iran sent counter-reply to US 15-point proposal; Lebanon banned Hezbollah military wing; IAEA Board emergency session. US $4/gal gas milestone. 11,000+ targets struck in Iran.
